scheme_name: National Programme for Prevention and Control of Cancer, Diabetes, Cardiovascular Diseases and Stroke (NPCDCS)
ministry_department: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
short_description: Free screening, diagnosis, and treatment for non-communicable diseases including cancer, diabetes, hypertension, and stroke.
detailed_description: NPCDCS is a comprehensive program to prevent and control major non-communicable diseases (NCDs) in India. The program provides free screening for diabetes, hypertension, and common cancers at primary health centers, followed by referral for diagnosis and treatment at district and tertiary level facilities. The program focuses on health promotion, early diagnosis, and management of NCDs through a three-tier system of NCD clinics.
key_benefits: Free screening for diabetes, hypertension, and common cancers (oral, breast, cervical)
Free diagnostic tests at government facilities
Subsidized treatment and medicines for diagnosed conditions
Referral to higher facilities for specialized care
age_criteria: 30 years and above (screening), all ages for treatment
other_conditions: Screening recommended for all adults above 30 years
Available at all government health facilities

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: Any document showing age above 30 for screening
If available, helps in better diagnosis and treatment

application_steps_offline: Visit nearest Primary Health Center, Community Health Center, or District Hospital
Register at NCD clinic (usually specific days/hours)
Get free screening for blood pressure, blood sugar, and common cancers
If abnormality detected, get referred for diagnostic tests
Receive treatment and medicines at government facility
Follow up as per doctor's advice for monitoring
Get lifestyle counseling and health education

submission_locations.address: Primary Health Center, Community Health Center, or District Hospital
submission_locations.working_days: Monday to Saturday (specific NCD clinic days)
submission_locations.notes: NCD clinics operate on specific days, check with local health facility for schedule

processing_time: Immediate screening, treatment as per diagnosis

faq.question: Who should get screened?
faq.answer: All adults above 30 years should get screened annually for diabetes, hypertension, and common cancers.

faq.question: Is treatment free?
faq.answer: Screening is free. Treatment and medicines are provided free or at subsidized rates at government facilities.

faq.question: What cancers are screened?
faq.answer: Common cancers screened include oral cancer, breast cancer (women), and cervical cancer (women).

tips_for_successful_application: Visit NCD clinic on designated days (check with local health facility)
Go for screening even if you feel healthy - early detection is key
Follow lifestyle advice given by health workers
target_beneficiaries: People with family history of NCDs
People with risk factors (obesity, smoking, etc.)
